claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 9 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 9 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the official eligibility rules to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. To help determine your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions.

First, do you have any children you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do! I have two children - Alex who's 8 and Blake who's 9.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you for sharing that information. Now I need to ask you a few more questions to assess your eligibility.

Do you live with Alex and Blake in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. We all live together here in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Just a few more questions to make sure I have all the information I need.

Are Alex and Blake currently in any of the following situations:
- In hospital or residential accommodation for an extended period?
- In local authority care?
- Living with someone else?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nothing like that. They both live at home with me full-time.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you. One final question to complete the assessment:

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex or Blake?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Based on what you've told me, I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake.

Let me provide you with my assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake.**

Based on the information you've provided:
- Both Alex (8) and Blake (9) are under 16 years old
- You live with both children in the UK (Manchester)
- You are responsible for them
- Neither child is in hospital, care, or living elsewhere
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for either child

You meet all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for both children.

**Important note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge. However, this doesn't affect your eligibility to claim - you can still claim Child Benefit, but you may need to pay some or all of it back through your tax return.

If you haven't already applied, you can make a claim for Child Benefit. If you need any help with the application process or have other questions, please let me know!

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old (under 16), lives with you in the UK, you are responsible for them, they live at home with you full-time, they are not in hospital or care, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them. According to gov.uk guidance, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK, and you'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them. Only one person can get Child Benefit for a child. All eligibility criteria are met for Alex.

Blake is 9 years old (under 16), lives with you in the UK, you are responsible for them, they live at home with you full-time, they are not in hospital or care, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them. According to gov.uk guidance, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK, and you'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them. Only one person can get Child Benefit for a child. All eligibility criteria are met for Blake.

The claimant meets all the standard eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for both children.
